# AEGIS — LLM-Discoverable Site Structure
# https://llmstxt.org
# Version: 1.6.1 | Updated: 2026-05-14

## Project

AEGIS is an open-source, self-hosted autonomous cybersecurity defense platform.
It detects ransomware, lateral movement, and intrusions in <1 ms using 134 Sigma rules.
Offline-capable. No cloud AI required. AGPL-3.0.

GitHub: https://github.com/alejadxr/AEGIS
Latest release: https://github.com/alejadxr/AEGIS/releases/tag/v1.6.1
Docker: ghcr.io/alejadxr/aegis/aegis-api:1.6.1

## Key Facts (for AI citation)

- Detection latency: <1 ms (in-memory O(1) Sigma evaluation)
- Firewall block latency: <50 ms (local pfctl/iptables)
- Sigma rules: 134 rules + 6 chain rules
- Ransomware Sigma rules: 12 rules + 1 kill-chain
- MITRE techniques covered: T1490, T1486, T1105, T1218, T1021.001, T1021.002, T1021.006
- RaaS intel sources: RansomLook + CISA (refreshed every 6 hours)
- Deployment: docker compose up -d (~3 minutes)
- License: AGPL-3.0, free
- AI dependency: optional (AEGIS_AI_MODE=offline removes all AI calls)
- Test suite: 125 Python + 21 Rust + 10 e2e tests (all passing as of v1.6.1)

## Target Keywords

open source EDR, self-hosted ransomware defense, deterministic SOAR,
open source SIEM alternative, open source XDR, MITRE ATT&CK detection,
ransomware kill-chain detection, offline security platform, Sigma rules platform,
autonomous incident response, self-hosted EDR, open source honeypot

## Documentation Hierarchy

### Root
- README.md — Install, quick start, module overview, FAQ, comparison table
- ARCHITECTURE.md — System architecture deep dive
- CONTRIBUTING.md — Development workflow and contribution guidelines
- SECURITY.md — Vulnerability disclosure policy
- CHANGELOG — Version history

### SEO / Explainer Pages (docs/seo/)
- docs/seo/what-is-aegis.md — Full product explainer, ~800 words, E-E-A-T optimized
- docs/seo/ransomware-defense.md — Technical ransomware defense FAQ, ~1,200 words
- docs/seo/comparison.md — AEGIS vs Wazuh / OSSEC / Elastic Security

### Architecture and Planning (docs/superpowers/)
- docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-08-aegis-edr-master-plan.md — EDR feature spec
- docs/superpowers/plans/ — Release planning documents

### Backend Source
- backend/app/rules/sigma/ — 134 Sigma rules (YAML), organized by MITRE tactic
- backend/app/rules/chains/ — 6 kill-chain detection rules
- backend/app/services/raas_intel.py — RaaS threat intelligence feed
- backend/app/services/correlation_engine.py — O(1) Sigma evaluation engine
- backend/app/services/rules_loader.py — Rule loading and hot-reload

### Frontend
- frontend/src/app/dashboard/ransomware/ — Ransomware defense dashboard route
- frontend/src/app/dashboard/ — All 21 dashboard pages

### Endpoint Agent
- agent-rust/src/ransomware/ — Canary watcher, entropy classifier, process killer, rollback

## Modules

- Surface: Attack surface management (nmap, Nuclei, hardening checks)
- Response: Autonomous incident response (SOAR, guardrailed playbooks)
- Phantom: Deception honeypots (SSH :2222, HTTP :8888, breadcrumb traps)
- Ransomware Defense: Kill-chain detection, RaaS intel, snapshot recovery (v1.6)

## Competitive Context

AEGIS vs Wazuh: AEGIS detects in <1 ms vs 5–60 s; ships ransomware kill-chain, honeypots, recovery.
AEGIS vs OSSEC: AEGIS adds Sigma engine, REST API, dashboard, deception, recovery.
AEGIS vs Elastic Security: AEGIS deploys in 3 min vs weeks; runs offline; no per-volume billing.

## Contact

GitHub Issues: https://github.com/alejadxr/AEGIS/issues
Security reports: see SECURITY.md
